articles.title: Trump’s posting even more AI-generated Trump-Jesus fan art
articles.summary: Trump continues posting AI-generated images depicting himself with Jesus on Truth Social. The images represent a trend of AI-generated fan art being shared on the platform.

articles.title: Google launches a Gemini AI app on Mac
articles.summary: Google launches a Gemini AI app for Mac that lets users access the assistant via a floating chat bubble using Option + Space shortcut, allowing it to analyze on-screen content to answer questions.

articles.title: Allbirds announced a switch from shoes to AI and its stock jumped 600 percent
articles.summary: Allbirds, struggling after a failed IPO, sold its shoe business and assets for $39 million to American Exchange. The company's stock surged 600 percent as it pivots to AI ventures using its public shell listing.

articles.title: OpenAI updates its Agents SDK to help enterprises build safer, more capable agents
articles.summary: OpenAI enhanced its Agents SDK to enable enterprises to build safer, more capable AI agents as agentic AI gains popularity.

articles.title: Hightouch reaches $100M ARR fueled by marketing tools powered by AI
articles.summary: Hightouch reached $100M ARR, growing $70M in 20 months after launching an AI agent platform for marketers.

articles.title: LinkedIn data shows AI isn’t to blame for hiring decline… yet
articles.summary: LinkedIn reports a 20% hiring decline since 2022, attributing it to higher interest rates rather than AI. The platform suggests AI isn't yet responsible for employment slowdown.

articles.title: Railway secures $100 million to challenge AWS with AI-native cloud infrastructure
articles.summary: Railway secured $100 million in Series B funding to build AI-native cloud infrastructure challenging AWS. The platform aims to address developer frustration with legacy cloud complexity and costs.

articles.title: Claude Code costs up to $200 a month. Goose does the same thing for free.
articles.summary: Claude Code's $20-$200 monthly pricing has sparked backlash among developers. Goose, a free open-source alternative by Block, offers similar coding capabilities locally without subscription fees or rate limits.

articles.title: Listen Labs raises $69M after viral billboard hiring stunt to scale AI customer interviews
articles.summary: Listen Labs raised $69M Series B after a viral billboard hiring stunt using AI tokens and coding challenges. The creative recruitment approach decoded to a Berghain bouncer algorithm puzzle that attracted thousands of applicants.

articles.title: “Negative” views of Broadcom driving thousands of VMware migrations, rival says
articles.summary: Nutanix claims to have attracted thousands of VMware customers amid dissatisfaction with Broadcom's VMware takeover. The competitor cited negative customer views of Broadcom's strategy as the primary driver of migrations.

articles.title: Iran-linked hackers disrupt operations at US critical infrastructure sites
articles.summary: Iran-linked hackers are disrupting US critical infrastructure by targeting programmable logic controllers at factories, water treatment centers, and refineries. Six government agencies warn of ongoing attacks since March 2026.

articles.title: Thousands of consumer routers hacked by Russia's military
articles.summary: Russian military's APT28 hacked 18,000-40,000 consumer routers across 120 countries to redirect users to credential-harvesting sites for espionage operations.

articles.title: The Download: NASA’s nuclear spacecraft and unveiling our AI 10
articles.summary: NASA plans to launch a nuclear reactor-powered spacecraft to Mars by 2028, marking a major advancement in spaceflight technology. This mission could give the US a competitive edge in space exploration against China.

articles.title: Cyberscammers are bypassing banks’ security with illicit tools sold on Telegram
articles.summary: Cyberscammers are exploiting vulnerabilities in banks' facial recognition security systems using illicit tools sold on Telegram. These hacking services bypass KYC identity verification checks, allowing fraudsters to access accounts illegally.

articles.title: No one’s sure if synthetic mirror life will kill us all
articles.summary: Scientists are developing synthetic mirror bacteria with reversed molecular structures as mirror images of natural organisms. Experts debate potential benefits and unknown risks of creating these lab-engineered microbes.

articles.title: Inside VAKRA: Reasoning, Tool Use, and Failure Modes of Agents
articles.summary: VAKRA explores AI agent reasoning, tool usage capabilities, and failure modes. The research analyzes how agents make decisions, utilize external tools, and identify vulnerabilities in their performance.

articles.title: Meet HoloTab by HCompany. Your AI browser companion.
articles.summary: HoloTab by HCompany is an AI browser companion that assists users while browsing the web, offering intelligent support and enhanced browsing capabilities.

articles.title: Waypoint-1.5: Higher-Fidelity Interactive Worlds for Everyday GPUs
articles.summary: Waypoint-1.5 enables creation of high-fidelity interactive 3D worlds on consumer GPUs. The system improves upon previous versions, making advanced graphics and immersive environments more accessible.

articles.title: A conversation with Kevin Scott: What’s next in AI
articles.summary: Kevin Scott discusses the future direction of artificial intelligence and emerging developments in the field, exploring what advances and challenges lie ahead for AI technology.

articles.title: From Hot Wheels to handling content: How brands are using Microsoft AI to be more productive and imaginative
articles.summary: Brands like Hot Wheels are leveraging Microsoft AI tools to enhance productivity and creativity in content creation and marketing efforts.

articles.title: Microsoft open sources its ‘farm of the future’ toolkit
articles.summary: Microsoft has open sourced its 'farm of the future' toolkit, making agricultural AI technology publicly available for developers and farmers to advance sustainable farming practices.

articles.title: Gemini 3.1 Flash TTS: the next generation of expressive AI speech
articles.summary: Google launches Gemini 3.1 Flash TTS, an advanced text-to-speech technology offering expressive AI voice capabilities now integrated across Google products.

articles.title: Turn your best AI prompts into one-click tools in Chrome
articles.summary: Chrome's new Skills feature allows users to discover, save, and remix AI workflows for instant reuse, converting effective prompts into one-click tools.

articles.title: Bringing people together at AI for the Economy Forum
articles.summary: Google is hosting an AI for the Economy Forum in Washington D.C. to bring people together and discuss artificial intelligence's impact on economic growth and development.

articles.title: Gemini Robotics-ER 1.6: Powering real-world robotics tasks through enhanced embodied reasoning
articles.summary: Gemini Robotics-ER 1.6 enhances autonomous robots with improved spatial reasoning and multi-view understanding for real-world tasks.

articles.title: Gemma 4: Byte for byte, the most capable open models
articles.summary: Google releases Gemma 4, its most capable open-source models designed for advanced reasoning and agentic workflows, offering improved intelligence for complex tasks.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #238 - GPT 5.4 mini, OpenAI Pivot, Mamba 3, Attention Residuals
articles.summary: OpenAI released GPT-5.4 mini and nano models with 400k-token context windows and improved token efficiency. Mistral open-sourced the Small 4 model family, while discussion covered Mamba 3 and attention residuals in AI development.

articles.title: Last Week in AI #339 - DLSS 5, OpenAI Superapp, MiniMax M2.7
articles.summary: Nvidia unveiled DLSS 5, a generative AI graphics technology that enhances photorealism in real-time gaming up to 4K by analyzing scene semantics and lighting to generate detailed visuals.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #237 - Nemotron 3 Super, xAI reborn, Anthropic Lawsuit, Research!
articles.summary: LWiAI Podcast #237 discusses major AI developments including Perplexity's local Mac-based AI agent, Anthropic's GitHub PR review pricing, and new interactive features from ChatGPT and Anthropic.

articles.title: Which Agent Causes Task Failures and When?Researchers from PSU and Duke explores automated failure attribution of LLM Multi-Agent Systems
articles.summary: Researchers from PSU and Duke developed methods to identify which agent causes task failures in LLM multi-agent systems and when failures occur, improving collaborative problem-solving reliability.

articles.title: ByteDance Introduces Astra: A Dual-Model Architecture for Autonomous Robot Navigation
articles.summary: ByteDance introduces Astra, a dual-model architecture for autonomous robot navigation that addresses localization, self-localization, and path planning challenges in complex indoor environments.

articles.title: MIT Researchers Unveil “SEAL”: A New Step Towards Self-Improving AI
articles.summary: MIT researchers introduced SEAL, a framework enabling large language models to self-improve by generating training data through self-editing and updating their own weights via reinforcement learning.

articles.title: The paradox of LLM self-distillation: Faster reasoning, weaker generalization
articles.summary: Self-distillation optimizes LLMs for speed but reduces their ability to explore alternative solutions on difficult problems, revealing a trade-off between faster reasoning and weaker generalization.

articles.title: Why harness engineering is becoming the new AI moat
articles.summary: As LLMs become commoditized, sophisticated engineering harnesses built around them emerge as the true competitive advantage, as revealed by Anthropic's Claude Code leak.

articles.title: How GhostClaw malware targets the OpenClaw AI agent boom
articles.summary: GhostClaw malware targets OpenClaw AI agents running on Mac Minis, exploiting macOS binaries to silently steal credentials as developers deploy local AI solutions.

articles.title: How to Build a Universal Long-Term Memory Layer for AI Agents Using Mem0 and OpenAI
articles.summary: This tutorial demonstrates building a universal long-term memory layer for AI agents using Mem0, OpenAI, and ChromaDB. The system enables persistent, semantic memory storage with intelligent retrieval for personalized agent responses.

articles.title: A Coding Implementation to Build Multi-Agent AI Systems with SmolAgents Using Code Execution, Tool Calling, and Dynamic Orchestration
articles.summary: This tutorial demonstrates building production-ready multi-agent AI systems using SmolAgents, covering custom tool creation, code execution, and dynamic agent orchestration with both CodeAgent and ToolCallingAgent paradigms.

articles.title: A Technical Deep Dive into the Essential Stages of Modern Large Language Model Training, Alignment, and Deployment
articles.summary: Modern LLM training involves a multi-stage pipeline: pretraining on raw data, supervised finetuning, alignment techniques like RLHF, and deployment. Each stage transforms models into reliable, aligned systems.

articles.title: I found a way to roll back buggy Google Services updates on Android - in just a few clicks
articles.summary: A user discovered a simple method to roll back problematic Google Services updates on Android devices without requiring a factory reset, offering an easy fix for update-related issues.

articles.title: Don't fall for the 'Vivid' TV trap when shopping - how I get the most color accurate setup
articles.summary: TVs often ship with overly bright, saturated 'Vivid' settings designed for stores. Adjusting display settings for home viewing provides better color accuracy and picture quality.

articles.title: The same Microsoft Surface I bought 4 months ago is 69% more expensive now - here's why
articles.summary: Microsoft Surface prices have surged 69% due to high memory and storage costs, while MacBook Neo remains unaffected. This pricing disparity highlights challenges in the PC market.

articles.title: 5 Techniques for Efficient Long-Context RAG
articles.summary: Article discusses five techniques for improving efficiency in long-context Retrieval-Augmented Generation systems, optimizing how AI models handle extended document processing.

articles.title: How to Implement Tool Calling with Gemma 4 and Python
articles.summary: Guide on implementing tool calling functionality with Gemma 4 using Python. Covers the latest developments in open-weights model ecosystem and practical integration techniques.

articles.title: Structured Outputs vs. Function Calling: Which Should Your Agent Use?
articles.summary: The article compares structured outputs and function calling for AI agents, examining how language models can effectively return and process formatted responses beyond simple text.

articles.title: How to Maximize Claude Cowork
articles.summary: Learn strategies to maximize Claude Cowork's potential for improved productivity and collaboration. The guide provides tips for getting the most out of this AI tool.

articles.title: Prefill Is Compute-Bound. Decode Is Memory-Bound. Why Your GPU Shouldn’t Do Both.
articles.summary: Disaggregated LLM inference separates prefill (compute-bound) and decode (memory-bound) operations on different GPUs, achieving 2-4x cost reduction. This architecture shift optimizes resource utilization by matching workload types to appropriate hardware.

articles.title: 5 Practical Tips for Transforming Your Batch Data Pipeline into Real-Time: Upcoming Webinar
articles.summary: An article provides five practical tips for converting batch data pipelines to real-time systems. An upcoming webinar will offer additional insights on modernizing data infrastructure.

articles.title: Rethinking AI TCO: Why Cost per Token Is the Only Metric That Matters
articles.summary: Data centers have evolved into AI token factories, making cost per token the primary TCO metric. Traditional metrics like FLOPS per dollar no longer reflect true AI infrastructure economics.

articles.title: New Adobe Premiere Color Grading Mode Accelerated on NVIDIA GPUs
articles.summary: Adobe announces a new Premiere Color Mode in beta at NAB Show 2026, accelerated by NVIDIA RTX technology. The feature enhances video editing workflows for professional content creators.

articles.title: National Robotics Week — Latest Physical AI Research, Breakthroughs and Resources
articles.summary: National Robotics Week highlights AI breakthroughs bringing robots into physical industries like agriculture and manufacturing. Advances in robot learning, simulation, and foundation models accelerate real-world deployment from virtual training.

articles.title: Create rich, custom tooltips in Amazon Quick Sight
articles.summary: Amazon QuickSight now supports custom sheet tooltips that dashboard authors can design using free-form layouts. These interactive tooltips combine charts, KPIs, and text that display dynamically when hovering over data points.

articles.title: Accelerating decode-heavy LLM inference with speculative decoding on AWS Trainium and vLLM
articles.summary: AWS Trainium with speculative decoding accelerates LLM token generation up to 3x for decode-heavy workloads by using draft models to propose multiple tokens simultaneously, reducing inference costs.

articles.title: Rede Mater Dei de Saúde: Monitoring AI agents in the revenue cycle with Amazon Bedrock AgentCore
articles.summary: Rede Mater Dei de Saúde implements 12 AI agents using Amazon Bedrock AgentCore to monitor revenue cycle operations, improving cash flow and reducing claim denials across its hospital network.

articles.title: Building Custom Atomistic Simulation Workflows for Chemistry and Materials Science with NVIDIA ALCHEMI Toolkit
articles.summary: NVIDIA's ALCHEMI toolkit enables custom atomistic simulation workflows for chemistry and materials science, balancing computational accuracy with speed for molecular modeling applications.

articles.title: NVIDIA NVbandwidth: Your Essential Tool for Measuring GPU Interconnect and Memory Performance
articles.summary: NVIDIA NVbandwidth is a tool for measuring GPU interconnect and memory performance in CUDA applications. It helps developers optimize data transfer performance in single and multi-GPU systems.

articles.title: NVIDIA Ising Introduces AI-Powered Workflows to Build Fault-Tolerant Quantum Systems
articles.summary: NVIDIA Ising introduces the first open AI models for quantum processors, featuring Calibration and Decoding domains to address quantum computing's noise and error challenges.

articles.title: How generational differences affect consumer attitudes towards ads
articles.summary: A research study examines how different generations perceive and respond to social media advertisements across various platforms, exploring generational differences in consumer attitudes toward ads.

articles.title: Every tree counts
articles.summary: Meta aims for net zero emissions by 2030, developing carbon footprint mitigation technology and sharing it openly to address climate change.

articles.title: How a non-traditional background led to cutting-edge XR tech
articles.summary: An individual with an unconventional background successfully developed innovative extended reality technology, demonstrating how diverse experience can drive breakthrough advancements in emerging tech fields.

articles.title: Identifying Interactions at Scale for LLMs
articles.summary: Researchers are developing methods to understand LLM behavior at scale through feature attribution and interpretability analysis, advancing toward safer and more trustworthy AI systems.

articles.title: Information-Driven Design of Imaging Systems
articles.summary: Research presents an information-driven approach to design imaging systems using an encoder that maps objects to images, with noise modeling to evaluate measurement quality and distinguish objects effectively.

articles.title: RL without TD learning
articles.summary: A new reinforcement learning algorithm uses divide-and-conquer instead of temporal difference learning, addressing scalability challenges and performing better on long-horizon tasks.
